purpose: Comprehensive ban list for the humanizer skill. Every item in this file must be eliminated from AI-generated text during the humanization process.
usage: In the Audit step, scan the rewritten draft against every entry in this file. In the Final Pass, replace or remove each match before delivering output.

banned_words.description: Single words that signal AI authorship. Replace with concrete, specific alternatives.

common_replacements.scalable: able to grow / built for scale

banned_phrases.description: Multi-word phrases that signal template-heavy AI writing. Remove or rewrite entirely.
phrases: At the end of the day
When all is said and done
I hope this finds you well
I hope this email finds you well
Please don't hesitate to contact me
Looking forward to hearing from you
I would like to take this opportunity
In light of the fact that
Take it to the next level

grammatical_tells.description: Structural and grammatical patterns that reveal AI authorship. Rewrite the sentence rather than doing a find-replace.

patterns.pattern: Em-dash used as a stylistic separator — like this
patterns.fix: Use a period, comma, or rewrite the sentence structure. One em-dash is acceptable; two in a paragraph is a tell.

patterns.pattern: Rule of Three: 'X, Y, and Z' groupings in every paragraph
patterns.fix: Vary list lengths. Use 2-item lists, 4-item lists, or prose instead of constant triads.
Rewrite as two separate sentences or choose the stronger point.

patterns.pattern: serves as / stands as / acts as
patterns.fix: State what it IS, not what it 'serves as'. Replace: 'it serves as a reminder' → 'it reminds us'
Just state the example and let it speak for itself.

patterns.pattern: Perfectly parallel bullet points of identical length
patterns.fix: Break the symmetry. Real humans write bullets of different lengths.
Name the subject specifically. 'This approach...' → 'The caching layer...' or 'It...'

patterns.pattern: Ending paragraphs with a forward-looking summary sentence
patterns.fix: End on a fact, observation, or implication — not a vague 'This will help achieve better outcomes.'

patterns.pattern: Overly hedged claims: 'It could be argued that', 'One might say'
patterns.fix: State the claim directly and own it. If uncertain, say so specifically: 'I'm not sure whether...'

patterns.pattern: Excessive passive voice: 'It was determined that', 'Steps were taken'
patterns.fix: Name who did what. 'The team decided' or 'We took three steps'

formatting_tells.description: Formatting choices that make text look AI-generated at a glance. Remove all of these.
items: Emojis in professional or analytical writing (🚀, ✅, 💡 used decoratively)
Title Case section subheadings inside prose (e.g. 'The Importance Of Clear Communication')
Bold headers inside bullet lists when bullets already have labels
Curly / smart quotes used inconsistently mixed with straight quotes
Every paragraph ending in '.' followed by a blank line with a transition sentence
Numbered lists for items that have no natural sequence
Excessive white space from double-blank-lines between every paragraph
Repeated use of 'Key takeaways:', 'In summary:', 'Important note:' callout boxes
Section labels like 'Introduction', 'Body', 'Conclusion' that don't add value

structural_boilerplate.description: Generic sections and frameworks that AI over-uses. Replace with content-specific structure.
opener_patterns_to_avoid: In recent years, [topic] has become increasingly important...
[Topic] is more important than ever before...

quality_checks.rhythmic_variety: No more than 2 sentences of similar length in a row. Mix short punchy sentences (5–10 words) with longer ones (20–30 words).
quality_checks.decisive_voice: No passive-voice hedging on the writer's own views. If the text expresses a view, own it.
quality_checks.grounded_clarity: Every abstract claim must be followed by a concrete example, number, or specific detail.
quality_checks.data_integrity: All statistics, dates, and facts must match the source material exactly. Never round aggressively or invent supporting data.
